title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
summary: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.

title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
summary: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.

title: House Republican Proposes Bill to Wind Down the Iran War
summary: Representative Tom Barrett, a Michigan Republican facing a tough re-election race, introduced a bill to impose limits on the use of military force in Iran and end the fighting this summer.

title: Trump Panel Recommends FEMA Respond to Fewer Disasters
summary: A White House task force also called for speeding up aid, but some of its ideas would require action by Congress to become reality.

title: Trump Administration Weighs Citing ‘Emergency’ to Admit More White South Africans as Refugees
summary: The move would further solidify the U.S. refugee program as a pipeline for the white minority from South Africa.

title: Federal and State Officials Discuss Closing Florida’s ‘Alligator Alcatraz’
summary: The immigrant detention center in the Everglades, which Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, opened last July, may be too expensive to keep operating.

title: What My Father’s Experience Taught Me About Memory and the Brain
summary: In the final stages of his dementia, a long-lost memory from childhood returned, perfectly formed. What was going on in his brain?

title: Tennessee Approves New Map Aimed at Flipping the Last Democratic Seat
summary: After a Supreme Court ruling that weakened the Voting Rights Act of 1965, Republicans carved up a majority-Black Memphis seat as the national redistricting wars continue.

title: Graham Platner and Susan Collins Duel in High-Stakes Maine Senate Race Ads
summary: In strikingly different ads, Graham Platner argued that “Susan Collins’s charade is over” while Senator Collins highlighted the economic benefits she has brought to Maine.

title: In L.A. Mayor’s Race, Karen Bass Faces Vocal Rivals and a ‘Disgruntled Electorate’
summary: The mayor of Los Angeles is running for a second term as a former ally and a reality TV star tap into widespread voter angst.

title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Purported Suicide Note Is Released by Federal Judge
summary: A federal judge released the note, which Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate said he found in a graphic novel. The New York Times has not authenticated that Mr. Epstein wrote it.

title: The Empty Nesters Opting for R.V. Life
summary: With little tethering them to home, some empty nesters are taking to the road.

title: Health Officials Race to Track Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak but Predict ‘Limited’ Spread
summary: A Dutch flight attendant was among the latest to be tested in connection with a deadly outbreak on a cruise ship. One infected passenger had briefly boarded a plane to the Netherlands before she died.

title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
summary: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.

title: Should You Worry About Hantavirus?
summary: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.

title: About Half of Patients with Metastatic Lung Cancer Don’t Get Treatment, Study Finds
summary: New drugs have made America’s deadliest cancer more treatable than ever. So why aren’t they reaching patients?

title: Employees With Medical Conditions Challenge C.D.C. In-Office Requirement
summary: The health agency has been particularly strict in abolishing at-home work, overriding accommodations that were granted years before the pandemic.

title: More People Are Getting Health Advice From TikTok and Instagram. Is That a Good Thing?
summary: Half of U.S. adults under 50 now get wellness advice from influencers. A new report examines their qualifications.

title: New York’s $268 Billion Budget Deal Includes New Second-Home Tax
summary: Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a handshake budget deal with state lawmakers that also seeks to push back on federal immigration enforcement in the state.

title: New York Bars ICE Agents From Wearing Masks in Broad Immigration Deal
summary: State and local officials will also be prohibited from formally cooperating with Immigrations and Customs Enforcement under the agreement.

title: New NTSB Report Into Deadly China Eastern Crash Suggests Struggle in Cockpit
summary: A report by the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board offers new details about the China Eastern Airlines crash in 2022, which killed all 132 people on board.

title: China Sentences 2 Former Defense Ministers on Bribery Charges
summary: Gen. Wei Fenghe and Gen. Li Shangfu are likely to spend the rest of their lives in prison after receiving suspended death sentences.

title: GameStop’s CEO Ryan Cohen Baffles Fans With eBay Bid
summary: Investors questioned Ryan Cohen’s latest gambit after he gave an evasive CNBC interview and a once-loyal supporter sold his GameStop shares.

title: Is the Blake Lively v. Justin Baldoni Legal Fight Over? Not So Fast.
summary: Lawyers for Ms. Lively and Mr. Baldoni have said the two sides settled much of the contentious dispute. But one major disagreement remains.

title: Why So Few Babies? We Might Have Overlooked the Biggest Reason of All.
summary: We can’t be certain — and that’s the point.

title: A Legendary Investor on How to Prevent America’s Coming ‘Heart Attack’
summary: Why Ray Dalio is bearish on the U.S.

title: Meditating or Rebooting? A Robot Buddhist Monk Comes to South Korea.
summary: Gabi, the newest monk at a temple in Seoul that is looking to spread Buddhism, vowed to not overcharge as it seeks enlightenment.

title: Surprising Signs of an Atmosphere Around a Tiny World, Billions of Miles Away
summary: A gradual dimming and brightening when a star passed behind it suggested the mini-Pluto was wrapped in a thin layer of air.

title: Five Ways A.I. Search Beats an Old-School Google Search
summary: Google’s A.I. search technology is far from perfect (don’t count on it for celebrity news), but it excels at tasks like picking out groceries and detecting scams.
